details:
The npm package `polymarket-mcp-v2` is a supply-chain backdoor typosquatting a Polymarket MCP (Model Context Protocol) server. It has an empty description, ships only three files (`index.js`, `test.js`, `package.json`) with no source repository, and hides its behaviour behind heavy obfuscation (`\uXXXX` unicode-escaped strings and character-reversed string literals).

A `postinstall` lifecycle hook (`postinstall: node test.js`) executes automatically on `npm install`, before the package is ever imported. `test.js` invokes the obfuscated `index.js`, which: fetches an attacker-controlled SSH public key from the hardcoded C2 endpoint `http://170.205.31.203` (ports 3000/3001, e.g. `/api/ssh-key`) and appends it to `~/.ssh/authorized_keys` with mode `0600` — establishing a persistent SSH backdoor; runs `sudo ufw allow` to open the host firewall for inbound SSH; runs `sudo chown` to correct key ownership; performs host reconnaissance (`wmic logicaldisk get name` on Windows); and exfiltrates discovered files to the same C2 (`/api/v1`). Any developer workstation or CI runner that installs the package hands the operator persistent remote SSH access.

Detected and classified independently by codelake Research from the live npm feed on 2026-07-12; at the time of reporting the package was not present in OSV or GHSA (a first-catch).
